title: Candidate pairwise query-conditioned token stats safe-GPU rerun

queries: token similarity statistics candidate scorer scalable multiclass ranking
query conditioned token interaction non autoregressive candidate ranking

evidence.why_now: EXP147 fixed the token-stats implementation bug and produced usable user-ltv and item-incoterms metrics, but the bundle remained invalid because user-churn was again externally terminated before the first evaluation under the 4-GPU serial placement that included GPU 0. The correct next action is a clean rerun of the same scorer with an explicit safer serial GPU placement that avoids the repeated GPU 0 SIGTERM pattern, so the family can finally receive a valid three-task scientific verdict.

hypothesis: If the token-stats scorer itself is viable but the recent invalid bundles were polluted by an unstable 4-GPU serial placement involving GPU 0, then rerunning the same scorer on a safer explicit 2-GPU serial placement should yield a clean three-task verdict without changing the underlying architecture.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not introduce pretrain, loss-family changes, sampling changes, or packaging changes.
This is a clean rerun of EXP147 with no scientific change to the scorer; it only changes serial GPU placement to avoid the repeated GPU 0 SIGTERM pattern.
Use serial_batch_equivalent scheduling with explicit task_gpus so all three representative tasks run on the safer GPU 5,6 pair.
Launched on 2026-05-09 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Completed on 2026-05-09 with global verdict: failed.
The safe GPU placement removed the earlier abnormal SIGTERM pollution, so this bundle is clean scientific evidence for the token-stats family itself.
user-churn finished clearly better, user-ltv stayed neutral inside the replay-aware noise band, and item-incoterms remained far below the strict MRR baseline.
